title: SECURITY FOR COSTS
description:
1. This application is brought by the defendant against plaintiff ,it acts as a remedy to the defendant before trial.
2. if the plaintiff fails to deposit security for costs ,the suit abets and it can be dismissed under order 26 rule 2 (1) of CPR 
3. if the suit is dismissed , the plaintiff can set aside the order with sufficent cause under order 26 rule 2 (2) CPR
4.  under order 26 rule 3 of civil procedure rules SI 71-1
procedure:
1. application is by chamber summons supported by an affidavit
2.make payments of court fees 
3. file in the relevant court registry thus court where the suit is pending .
locusClassicus: GM Combined vs AK Detergents (u) ltd SCCA no.34/1995

title: APPLICATION FOR GARNISHEE
description: APPLICATION FOR GARNISHEE ORDER
parties: UNIQUE HOLDINGS LTD Versus BUSINESS SKILLS TRUST LTD
court: BY JUSTICE MADRAMA (as he was then )
procedure:
1. file an application for a garnishee order nisi under order 23 rule 10 CPR by chamber summons supported by an affidavit ,accompanied by proof of payment.
2. file the application in the relevant court registry .
3. under order 23rule 1 CPR ,application will be heard exparte and if court is satisfied that not all issue a Garnishee order Nisi.
4.the order to be served on the bank it saves to freeze the account till proceeding is concluded.
5.file a return of service in according with order 5 rule 16 CPR
6. If bank fails to show cause why money shouldn't be paid to the judgement debtor ,the order should be made absolute.
7. order absolute compels the bank to pay the money on the account of judgment creditor.(order 23 rule 7)
locusClassicus: under section 38(c) CIVIL PROCEEDING ACT  and Order 23 rule 1 CIVIL PROCEEDURE RULE

title: CONTEMPT OF COURT ORDERS
description:
CONDITIONS FOR CONTEMPT 
1. there exists a lawful order 
2. the party is aware or has knowledge of the lawful order
3. the order has been violated 
4. the party had the ability to comply
parties: MEGA INDUSTRIES LTD Vs COMFORM UG LTD
procedure: Section 37 of the Judicature Cap 16 , Section 98 of the CPA, Order 23 RULES 1 & 10 OF THEE Civil Procedure Rules.
locusClassicus: Florence Dawaru vs Angumale ALBINO& Another (HCMA NO.0096 OF 2016) where Justice Mubiru defined it as any conduct that scandalizes the court, prejudices judical proceedings , or interferes with the adminstration of Justice

title: AFFIDAVIT OF SERVICE
description: This is proof of service to be filed and showed to court
procedure:
1. service of legal document on  the other party
2. draft the affidavit of service 
3.attach a copy of the received legal document  having signature or stamp
locusClassicus: Order 5 rule 16 of the CIVIL PROCEDURE RULES

title: AMENDMENT OF PLEADINGS
description:
Order 6 rule 19 of CPR provides that court may at anystage of proceedings allow any of the parties to amend their proceedings .
GENERAL RULE ; is that all parties are bound by their pleadings .
there are 3 ways to amend 1. within time 2.consent of parties 3. leave of court
parties: INTERFREIGHT FORWARDERS (U)LTD Vs EAST AFRICAN DEV'T BANK
procedure:
unless its barred by law ....its by chamber summons ,supported by the affidavit 
forum -is the same court u filed the claim.
locusClassicus: GASCO TRANSPORT BUS Co.LTD Vs MARTIN ADALA OBENE SCCA no. 2 of 2001(it laid down the principles )

title: TEMPORARY INJUNCTION
description:
section 42 of the judicature ACT empowers the High Court to grant an injunction to restrain any person from doing any act as maybe specified by High Court.
order 41 rule 1(a) civil procedure rules allows court to grant injunction if the property is in danger of being damaged,wasted by any party in the suit.
procedure:
1. prepare pleadings after instituting the suit ,order 14 rule 9 of civil procedure rules provide that the procedure is by summons in chambers and supported by an affidavit .
2. prepare documents for assessment and make payment of court fees
3. file in the relevant court registry
locusClassicus: STANBIC BANK LTD VS THE COMMISSIONER GENERAL URA HCMA No.42/2010 which court noted that temporary injunction is to protect the plaintiff against injury by variation of his/her rights of which they couldnt be compensated in damages ,recoverable in reaction.

title: ENGAGEMENT LETTER
description:
section 50 of the Advocates Act Cap 267 provides or permits advocates to make agreements with their clients as to their remuneration regarding any contentous business done .
Reg 2 of the Advocates (professional Conduct ) Regulations states that no advocate shall act for any person unless HE/SHE  has received instructions from his client.
parties: PETER JOGO BABU VS PETER LONGI
court: High Court of Uganda holden at ARUA
locusClassicus: PETER JOGO BABU VS PETER LONGI as cited above noted that the letter ought to be signed by both parties.
